players.name: Kim Seung-gyu
players.bio: A veteran shot-stopper with 87 caps, Kim Seung-gyu has been Korea's reliable last line of defence for over a decade. Now plying his trade in Japan with FC Tokyo, the 35-year-old brings World Cup experience and steady hands to Canada/Mexico/USA 2026.
players.facts: Has kept goal for Korea across three different decades — 2010s, 2020s, and now the 2026 tournament.

players.name: Lee Han-beom
players.bio: A composed young defender who swapped the K-League for Danish football with Midtjylland, Lee Han-beom is one of Korea's brightest defensive prospects. With just 8 caps at 23, this World Cup could be his coming-out party on the grandest stage.
players.facts: Born in 2002, Lee Han-beom could be one of the youngest defenders in the Korea squad at WC2026.

players.name: Lee Gi-hyuk
players.bio: A home-grown midfielder with Gangwon FC, Lee Gi-hyuk brings energy and versatility to Korea's engine room. With only 3 caps to his name, this World Cup represents a huge leap for the 25-year-old K-League talent.
players.facts: Lee Gi-hyuk plays for Gangwon FC — a club whose home stadium sits near the scenic Korean mountains.

players.name: Kim Min-jae
players.bio: The Monster. Kim Min-jae is one of the world's elite centre-backs, dominating aerial duels and driving out of defence with authority. A Champions League competitor with Bayern Munich, the 29-year-old is Korea's defensive cornerstone and a genuine global superstar.
players.facts: Nicknamed 'The Monster' for his physicality — opponents reportedly find him as fun to face as his nickname suggests.

players.name: Kim Tae-hyeon
players.bio: A solid defensive presence who made the move to Kashima Antlers in Japan, Kim Tae-hyeon is steadily building his international career with 7 caps. The 25-year-old is a disciplined defender who reads the game well beyond his years.
players.facts: Kim Tae-hyeon plays in Japan's J1 League — making him one of several Koreans keeping tabs on their rivals next door.

players.name: Hwang In-beom
players.bio: A box-to-box dynamo with 73 caps, Hwang In-beom brings relentless energy and European pedigree from Feyenoord to Korea's midfield. A key creative force, he's equally dangerous breaking forward as he is winning the ball back.
players.facts: Hwang In-beom has played in Russia, Belgium, and the Netherlands — a proper midfield globetrotter.

players.name: Son Heung-min
players.bio: Korea's greatest ever. Son Heung-min arrives at his fourth World Cup as captain and icon, with 56 international goals from 144 caps. Now with LAFC in the host nation's own backyard, Sonny remains the heart, soul, and brilliant left foot of the Taegeuk Warriors.
players.facts: Son is Korea's all-time top scorer. He won the Premier League Golden Boot in 2022 — shared with Mohamed Salah.

players.name: Paik Seung-ho
players.bio: A technically gifted midfielder now playing in the English Championship with Birmingham City, Paik Seung-ho offers creativity and set-piece quality. With 27 caps and 3 international goals, the 29-year-old is a reliable option in Korea's midfield rotation.
players.facts: Paik Seung-ho once played in La Liga with Villarreal — not bad for a lad from South Korea.

players.name: Cho Gue-sung
players.bio: Cho Gue-sung became a household name at Qatar 2022 with two headers against Ghana in one of the tournament's great individual displays. The powerful Midtjylland striker boasts 12 goals in 44 caps and will be hungry to add to his World Cup legend.
players.facts: His brace against Ghana at Qatar 2022 made him a viral sensation overnight — and a heartthrob across South Korea.

players.name: Lee Jae-sung
players.bio: A seasoned campaigner with 105 caps and 15 goals, Lee Jae-sung is the tireless Bundesliga midfielder who never stops running. A Qatar 2022 veteran, his industry and intelligence in the Mainz 05 engine room make him indispensable to Korea's midfield.
players.facts: With 105 caps, Lee Jae-sung is one of Korea's most-capped outfield players — a true workhorse of a generation.

players.name: Hwang Hee-chan
players.bio: A livewire forward from Chuncheon, Hwang Hee-chan has been a consistent threat for both Wolves and Korea with 17 goals in 79 caps. Quick, direct and relentless in the press, the 30-year-old is the perfect foil for Son Heung-min in attack.
players.facts: Scored the winner against Portugal at Qatar 2022 that sent Korea into the last 16 — pure pandemonium in 90+1'.

players.name: Song Bum-keun
players.bio: Jeonbuk Hyundai Motors' trusted stopper, Song Bum-keun is a dependable K-League goalkeeper who has earned his three Korea caps through consistent club form. Born in Sangju, the 28-year-old is a proud homegrown talent in the squad.
players.facts: Song Bum-keun is one of the few players in the squad whose entire career has been in the K-League.

players.name: Lee Tae-seok
players.bio: A dynamic young left-back from Seoul, Lee Tae-seok has impressed enough at Austria Wien to earn 15 caps and even a senior international goal at just 23. One of Korea's most exciting full-back prospects, WC2026 could define his career trajectory.
players.facts: Lee Tae-seok plays in Austria — a country better known for Mozart than marauding full-backs, until now.

players.name: Cho Wi-je
players.bio: A raw but promising defender from Jeonbuk Hyundai Motors, Cho Wi-je earned his first senior cap and has the pedigree of a player coaches believe in for the future. At 24 with 1 cap, a World Cup squad spot is already a remarkable achievement.
players.facts: Cho Wi-je has just 1 senior cap — making this World Cup potentially his biggest stage by a very large margin.

players.name: Kim Moon-hwan
players.bio: A dependable right-back from Hwaseong with 35 caps, Kim Moon-hwan has been a trusted defensive option for Korea across several years. Now at Daejeon Hana Citizen, he brings experience, solidity, and no-nonsense defending to the squad.
players.facts: Kim Moon-hwan's K-League career has taken him across multiple clubs — a journeyman defender who always finds a home.

players.name: Park Jin-seob
players.bio: A tough-tackling defensive midfielder from Jeonju, Park Jin-seob has made 14 appearances for Korea and even chipped in with an international goal. Currently in China's Super League with Zhejiang, he's the unglamorous but essential midfield shield.
players.facts: Park Jin-seob plays in China's Super League — one of the more unusual career paths in this Korea squad.

players.name: Bae Jun-ho
players.bio: One of the most exciting young talents in Korean football, Bae Jun-ho burst onto the European scene at Stoke City. The Daegu-born attacking midfielder is only 22 but already has 13 caps and 2 goals — WC2026 could be his true global introduction.
players.facts: Born in 2003, Bae Jun-ho is among the youngest in the squad and plays in the English Championship at just 22.

players.name: Oh Hyeon-gyu
players.bio: A powerful centre-forward from Namyangju who made the bold move to Beşiktaş in Turkey, Oh Hyeon-gyu has 6 goals in 27 caps and an eye for the spectacular. At 25, he's developing into a genuine international striker of real quality.
players.facts: Oh Hyeon-gyu made his name at Celtic before heading to Turkey — a striker who likes a dramatic destination.

players.name: Lee Kang-in
players.bio: A prodigious talent from Incheon, Lee Kang-in has been turning heads since winning the FIFA U-20 World Cup Golden Ball in 2019. The PSG attacking midfielder brings flair, vision and 11 international goals to Korea's creative arsenal at just 25.
players.facts: Won the Golden Ball at the 2019 U-20 World Cup — aged just 18, playing for Korea against the world's best.

players.name: Yang Hyun-jun
players.bio: A pacy right winger from Busan who has thrived in Scottish football with Celtic, Yang Hyun-jun earned his stripes in the Champions League before making his mark internationally. Quick, direct and increasingly confident, he's a genuine threat on the flank.
players.facts: Yang Hyun-jun has played Champions League football with Celtic — not a bad preparation for a World Cup debut.

players.name: Jo Hyeon-woo
players.bio: A commanding goalkeeper who has been Ulsan HD's rock for years, Jo Hyeon-woo brings 48 international caps and vast experience as Korea's dependable backup. The 34-year-old is a seasoned professional who keeps the No.1 honest in training.
players.facts: Jo Hyeon-woo has 48 caps but remains the backup — a reminder of just how competitive the Korean goalkeeping ranks are.

players.name: Seol Young-woo
players.bio: A cool, composed centre-back who has found his European adventure at Red Star Belgrade in Serbia, Seol Young-woo has accumulated 34 caps with a string of impressive defensive performances. Reliable and positionally astute at just 27.
players.facts: Seol Young-woo plays at Red Star Belgrade — a club with a passionate fanbase that would terrify most defenders.

players.name: Jens Castrop
players.bio: Born in Germany and representing Korea, Jens Castrop is one of football's most intriguing dual-identity stories. The Borussia Mönchengladbach defender chose the Taegeuk Warriors over Die Mannschaft and brings Bundesliga quality to Korea's backline at just 22.
players.facts: German-born Castrop chose to represent South Korea over Germany — one of football's more surprising international pledges.

players.name: Kim Jin-gyu
players.bio: A versatile midfielder with K-League pedigree, Kim Jin-gyu has contributed 3 goals in 22 caps for Korea from his Jeonbuk Hyundai Motors base. A reliable squad player with a taste for the big occasion, he brings domestic know-how to the international setup.
players.facts: Kim Jin-gyu is one of a trio of Jeonbuk Hyundai Motors players in this Korea squad — a club punching above its weight.

players.name: Eom Ji-sung
players.bio: A creative young midfielder making waves in the Championship with Swansea City, Eom Ji-sung has 2 goals in 9 caps and a natural flair for the game. The 24-year-old is one of the fresh faces bringing new energy to the Taegeuk Warriors in 2026.
players.facts: Eom Ji-sung plays in Wales with Swansea City — not quite the Korean peninsula, but he's making it work.

players.name: Lee Dong-gyeong
players.bio: A technically gifted midfielder with Ulsan HD, Lee Dong-gyeong has contributed an impressive 4 goals in just 18 international appearances — a fine return for a player still establishing himself at this level. An exciting wildcard option for Korea's attack.
players.facts: Lee Dong-gyeong averages a goal roughly every 4-5 caps — a remarkably sharp return for a squad midfielder.
